title: Top of the Mark Restaurant - San Francisco, CA | OpenTable
content: San Francisco's #1 Hotel Bar with legendary views - Top of the Mark, San Francisco, CA. Number 1 in California and San Francisco, and 2nd in the nation on USAToday's 10 Best Hotel Bars. Nestled on the 19th floor of the historic InterContinental Mark Hopkins, Top of the Mark's breathtaking panoramic views of San Francisco's skyline and iconic landmarks, including the majestic Golden Gate Bridge, the enigmatic Alcatraz, Union Square, and more, will leave you spellbound. What's more, we offer live entertainment each Saturday evening, 8-11 pm, where you can let the music move you on the dance floor ($15 cover pp applies). This menu from Top of the Mark. Top of the Mark reviews from people who’ve dined at this restaurant. Top of the Mark is celebrated for its "fabulous views" of San Francisco, creating an iconic city experience. San Francisco's top luxury hotels are profiled, with a spotlight on the iconic Top of the Mark rooftop lounge atop the Mark Hopkins hotel in Nob Hill.

title: TOP OF THE MARK, San Francisco - Nob Hill - Tripadvisor
content: A Must-do San Francisco Experience ... This bar is famous for a good reason! Spectacular views, great drinks and an attentive staff. We were staying at the Mark

title: Top of the Mark. Cocktails and spectacular city views!
content: 3. Top of the Mark. # Top of the Mark Cocktails in the clouds. A trip to the Top of the Mark, the restaurant/bar at the top of the Mark Hopkins Hotel on Nob Hill, is one of the quintessential San Francisco experiences; everyone should do it at least once! City view from table at Top of the Mark in San FranciscoView of the Bay and the City. ## History of the Top of the Mark. The **Mark Hopkins Hotel** is one of the Grand Dames of San Francisco, catering to the rich and famous of the 1920s, 30s and 40s, and sitting on top of Nob Hill since 1926. One of San Francisco's most elegant hotels was built on the site of the former mansion in 1926 and became one of the most prestigious hotels of the city, along with the Fairmont Hotel across the street. More on the history of the hotel.
